entries.description: Roy singlehandedly blocks $19 billion bipartisan disaster relief bill via objection during a pro forma session, delaying $900 million for hurricane-damaged Puerto Rico and funds for Texans still recovering from Hurricane Harvey; bill had already passed the Senate 85-8 and the House 257-150; Roy blamed procedural objections while communities waited for recovery aid
entries.causation_evidence: Congressional Record, May 24, 2019; Texas Tribune reporting; Senate roll call 85-8

entries.description: Roy votes against restoring back pay to 800,000 federal workers affected by the 2018-2019 government shutdown — one of only 7 House members to do so; bill passed 411-7 and was signed by Trump
entries.causation_evidence: House roll call vote record; GovTrack.us

entries.description: Roy votes against H.R. 6, the American Dream and Promise Act, which would have provided a path to legal status for approximately 2.5 million DACA recipients and Temporary Protected Status holders, many of whom have lived in the U.S. for decades
entries.causation_evidence: House roll call vote; GovTrack.us; Roy official voting record

entries.description: Roy consistently votes to repeal the Affordable Care Act, supporting multiple Texas-led federal lawsuits seeking to eliminate the ACA entirely; the TX-21 district alone has 340,700 residents with pre-existing conditions who would lose protections under a full ACA repeal
entries.causation_evidence: DCCC district analysis; multiple ACA repeal vote records (2019-2022); Texas lawsuit supporting votes

entries.description: During a House Judiciary hearing on violence against Asian Americans — convened after the Atlanta spa shootings killed 8 people, 6 of them Asian women — Roy invokes lynching imagery, saying 'There's old sayings in Texas about find all the rope in Texas and get a tall oak tree'; remarks condemned by Asian American members of Congress and Texas Democratic Party as a white supremacist dog whistle; Roy refuses to apologize, doubles down saying 'I meant it'
entries.causation_evidence: C-SPAN hearing footage; Rolling Stone; Texas Monthly; Rep. Grace Meng floor statement; Texas Democratic Party statement

entries.description: Roy votes against the bipartisan ALLIES Act, which would have increased by 8,000 the number of special immigrant visas for Afghan allies of the U.S. military; bill passes 407-16; within weeks, the Taliban retakes Kabul and thousands of Afghan allies who had assisted U.S. forces are left behind or endangered
entries.causation_evidence: House roll call vote record July 2021; subsequent fall of Kabul August 2021; GovTrack.us

entries.description: Roy is fined for repeatedly refusing to wear a mask on the House floor during the COVID-19 pandemic in summer 2021, in deliberate violation of House rules intended to protect fellow members and staff; part of a pattern of publicly undermining pandemic safety measures
entries.causation_evidence: GovTrack.us legislative profile; House press accounts (summer 2021)

entries.description: Roy publicly states he wants '18 more months of chaos and the inability to get stuff done' during the Democratic-controlled Congress and presidency; audio recording surfaces of the remark; statement reveals deliberate intent to prevent governance rather than legislate in the public interest
entries.causation_evidence: Recording surfaced July 2021; Texas Tribune reporting; multiple news outlets

entries.description: Roy is one of only 3 members of Congress (out of 435) to vote against the Emmett Till Antilynching Act, which passed 422-3; the bill named for a 14-year-old Black child tortured and murdered in 1955 would have made lynching a federal hate crime; Roy calls the bill 'an effort to advance a woke agenda'
entries.causation_evidence: House roll call vote March 2022; Roy official press release; Rolling Stone; Newsweek

entries.description: Roy votes against the Farm Workforce Modernization Act (H.R. 1603), which would have created a path to legal status for approximately 1.5 million undocumented agricultural workers who supply the U.S. food chain; bill had broad bipartisan agricultural industry support
entries.causation_evidence: House roll call vote record; Roy official voting record page

entries.description: Roy votes against the Bipartisan Safer Communities Act, the most significant federal gun safety legislation in nearly 30 years, passed in the aftermath of the Uvalde, Texas school massacre that killed 19 children and 2 teachers in Roy's own state; bill closes the 'boyfriend loophole,' strengthens background checks for under-21 buyers, and funds mental health programs
entries.causation_evidence: Senate roll call record; House vote record; GovTrack.us; context of Uvalde shooting May 24, 2022

entries.description: Roy plays key role in House Freedom Caucus blocking Kevin McCarthy's speakership for 15 rounds over 4 days in January 2023, requiring unprecedented 15 ballots before a speaker is elected; causes days of legislative paralysis and damages international perception of U.S. governance stability
entries.causation_evidence: Congressional Record January 2023; Texas Monthly; Texas Tribune; multiple national outlets

entries.description: Roy spearheads Freedom Caucus opposition to the $1.59 trillion bipartisan government funding deal, threatening to oust Speaker Mike Johnson and repeatedly bringing the government to the brink of shutdown; fiscal brinksmanship creates economic uncertainty, threatens federal employee pay, and disrupts essential government services
entries.causation_evidence: Texas Tribune (January 12, 2024); multiple reporting on Johnson speakership threats

entries.description: Roy leads a 'Dear Colleague' letter signed by 20 House conservatives demanding elimination of the enhanced federal Medicaid match for ACA expansion states; his proposed cuts, per CBO preliminary analysis, would cause at least 8.6 million Americans to lose health insurance coverage over 10 years; Roy frames the proposed coverage losses as 'transformative reform'
entries.causation_evidence: The Hill (May 1, 2025); CBO preliminary analysis cited in PBS NewsHour (May 12, 2025); NPR (May 9, 2025)

entries.description: Roy signs and circulates a letter to Congress that is later revealed to have been copied nearly verbatim from a Koch network-funded lobbying group (FreedomWorks-affiliated); the letter advocates for Medicaid cuts affecting millions of low-income Americans; watchdog group Accountable.US calls it 'shameless kowtowing to conservative billionaires'
entries.causation_evidence: Common Dreams (May 5, 2025); Accountable.US statement; letter comparison published by investigative reporters

entries.description: Roy publicly calls for Republicans to dismantle ACA healthcare subsidies entirely, stating 'Republicans in Congress suck on this' for not going far enough in repealing Obamacare; Roy describes the ACA as 'socialized medicine' and calls for eliminating all pandemic-era health care subsidies that provide coverage to millions of low-income Americans
entries.causation_evidence: The Hill (October 9, 2025); Roy Wall Street Journal op-ed

entries.description: Roy introduces the MAMDANI Act (Measures Against Marxism's Dangerous Adherents and Noxious Islamists Act), legislation to denaturalize and deport individuals based on political ideology and religious identity; legal scholars and civil liberties groups describe the bill as facially unconstitutional, targeting ideological and religious communities in violation of the First Amendment
entries.causation_evidence: Roy official press release; H.R. 8387 text; GovTrack.us bill record

entries.description: Roy introduces legislation to challenge Plyler v. Doe, the 1982 Supreme Court decision guaranteeing undocumented children the right to a free K-12 public education; overturning the ruling would strip educational rights from an estimated 750,000 undocumented school-age children currently enrolled in U.S. public schools
entries.causation_evidence: Roy official press release on Plyler v. Doe hearing (2026); H.R. filing; Congress.gov

entries.description: Roy votes against the NATO Support Act, which passed 357-22, that would have prevented the President from unilaterally withdrawing the U.S. from NATO without Senate consent; bill was designed to protect a foundational post-WWII collective defense alliance
entries.causation_evidence: House roll call vote record January 2019; GovTrack.us

entries.description: Roy initially encourages Trump's chief of staff to produce 'evidence' that the 2020 election was stolen, lending credibility to the stolen election narrative before ultimately certifying the election; his early encouragement of evidence claims contributed to the atmosphere leading to the January 6 Capitol insurrection, even though he later condemned Trump's behavior as potentially impeachable and voted to certify
entries.causation_evidence: Texas Monthly profile; Texas Tribune reporting on Roy's January 6 conduct

entries.description: Roy votes for and supports the 'Big Beautiful Bill' reconciliation package that includes work requirements and eligibility restrictions projected by CBO to remove millions from Medicaid and SNAP; Roy explicitly pushes for deeper cuts than what was in the bill, calling the Medicaid work requirements a 'joke' for not going far enough
entries.causation_evidence: PBS NewsHour (May 2025); NPR; NBC News; Roy statements on X (formerly Twitter)

entries.description: Roy consistently votes against climate legislation, environmental protections, and clean energy investment throughout his tenure; opposes the Inflation Reduction Act's clean energy provisions and describes them as 'green new deal subsidies'; his district in Central Texas is among regions increasingly exposed to extreme heat, drought, and wildfire risk
entries.causation_evidence: GovTrack.us voting record; Roy official statements on IRA; climate risk analyses for Central Texas

entries.description: Roy is one of only five Texas House Republicans to vote to certify the 2020 presidential election results, resisting intense pressure from Trump and MAGA allies; he had previously called Trump's behavior on January 6 potentially impeachable; his certification vote was a politically costly act of institutional integrity
entries.causation_evidence: Congressional Record January 7, 2021; Texas Tribune; multiple news outlets

entries.description: Roy co-sponsors and helps pass the Paycheck Protection Program Flexibility Act (H.R. 6886) during the COVID-19 pandemic, giving small businesses more time and flexibility to use PPP loans and qualify for forgiveness; bill provides critical relief to struggling small businesses
entries.causation_evidence: Congress.gov bill record; GovTrack.us

entries.description: Roy forces a recorded voice vote on January 5, 2021 on the seating of 67 representatives-elect from six contested states, publicly exposing the hypocrisy of colleagues who contested presidential results in those same states but not their own House races — a genuine act of procedural accountability that drew praise from across the political spectrum
entries.causation_evidence: Congressional Record; Washington Post editorial praising the move as a 'gutsy step'
